# enterotypes
# libs

```{r}
# Load libraries
library(phyloseq)
library(tidyverse)
library(vegan)
library(cluster)
library(fpc)
library(ggalluvial)
```

# main
```{r}

# ============================================================
# Load phyloseq
# ============================================================
ps <- readRDS('/home/sonec_iv/RSF_res_4_ps/RSF_all_physeq_wgs_COUNTS_v2.rds')

# ============================================================================
# ENTEROTYPE ANALYSIS FOR HUMAN GUT MICROBIOME (FINAL)
# ============================================================================
# Assumptions:
#   - phyloseq object named 'ps'
#   - sample_data contains: SampleID, Group (A/B/C), Timepoint (character: "-1", "-2")
#   - SampleID encodes patient ID and timepoint, e.g., "A51-1" or "AO-201-2"
# Outputs saved in folder "enterotype_analysis"
# ============================================================================

# ----------------------------- 0. Setup -------------------------------------
output_dir <- "enterotype_analysis"
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) dir.create(output_dir)

log_file <- file.path(output_dir, "analysis_log.txt")
sink(log_file, split = TRUE)
cat("Enterotype analysis started at:", date(), "\n\n")

# Load libraries
library(phyloseq)
library(tidyverse)
library(vegan)
library(cluster)
library(fpc)
library(ggalluvial)

# ----------------------------- 1. Parse SampleID ----------------------------
cat("Parsing SampleID to extract SubjectID and Timepoint...\n")
sample_df <- sample_data(ps) %>% data.frame()

sample_df <- sample_df %>%
  mutate(
    Timepoint_parsed = as.numeric(str_extract(SampleID, "-\\d+$")),
    SubjectID = str_replace(SampleID, "-\\d+$", "")
  )
sample_df$Timepoint <- as.character(sample_df$Timepoint_parsed)
sample_data(ps) <- sample_df
ps <- prune_samples(!is.na(sample_data(ps)$SubjectID) & !is.na(sample_data(ps)$Timepoint), ps)
cat("Number of samples after parsing:", nsamples(ps), "\n")

# ----------------------------- 2. Data preparation --------------------------
cat("Agglomerating to genus, converting to relative abundances, filtering...\n")
ps_genus <- tax_glom(ps, taxrank = "Genus", NArm = FALSE)
ps_rel <- transform_sample_counts(ps_genus, function(x) x / sum(x))
prev_threshold <- 0.1
ps_filt <- filter_taxa(ps_rel, function(x) sum(x > 0) > (prev_threshold * nsamples(ps_rel)), prune = TRUE)

abund <- as(otu_table(ps_filt), "matrix")
if (taxa_are_rows(ps_filt)) abund <- t(abund)
dist_mat <- vegdist(abund, method = "bray")
cat("Final abundance matrix dimensions:", dim(abund), "\n")

# ----------------------------- 3. Clustering indices ------------------------
k_range <- 2:6
set.seed(123)

cat("\nComputing clustering indices...\n")
pam_sil <- sapply(k_range, function(k) {
  pam_fit <- pam(dist_mat, k = k)
  mean(silhouette(pam_fit)[, 3])
})

ch_index <- sapply(k_range, function(k) {
  pam_fit <- pam(dist_mat, k = k)
  stats <- cluster.stats(dist_mat, pam_fit$clustering)
  ifelse(is.null(stats$ch), NA, stats$ch)
})

db_index <- sapply(k_range, function(k) {
  pam_fit <- pam(dist_mat, k = k)
  stats <- cluster.stats(dist_mat, pam_fit$clustering)
  ifelse(is.null(stats$davies.bouldin), NA, stats$davies.bouldin)
})

hc <- hclust(dist_mat, method = "ward.D2")
hc_sil <- sapply(k_range, function(k) {
  clusters <- cutree(hc, k = k)
  mean(silhouette(clusters, dist_mat)[, 3])
})

# Convert to numeric
pam_sil <- as.numeric(pam_sil)
ch_index <- as.numeric(ch_index)
db_index <- as.numeric(db_index)
hc_sil <- as.numeric(hc_sil)

# Normalize and combine
index_df <- data.frame(
  k = k_range,
  Silhouette = pam_sil,
  Calinski_Harabasz = ch_index / max(ch_index, na.rm = TRUE),
  Davies_Bouldin = db_index / max(db_index, na.rm = TRUE),
  Hierarchical_Sil = hc_sil
)

index_long <- index_df %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -k, names_to = "Index", values_to = "Value")

p_indices <- ggplot(index_long, aes(x = k, y = Value, color = Index, group = Index)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = k_range) +
  labs(title = "Comparison of clustering indices",
       x = "Number of clusters (k)", y = "Normalized score") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "clustering_indices_comparison.pdf"), p_indices, width = 8, height = 5)
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "clustering_indices_comparison.png"), p_indices, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)

# Choose optimal k (silhouette) – you can manually set to 3 if needed
best_k_sil <- k_range[which.max(pam_sil)]
cat("\nOptimal k by silhouette width:", best_k_sil, "(value:", round(max(pam_sil),4), ")\n")
cat("All silhouette widths:\n")
for (i in seq_along(k_range)) cat("  k =", k_range[i], ":", round(pam_sil[i],4), "\n")
chosen_k <- best_k_sil   # change to 3 if literature suggests
cat("Using k =", chosen_k, "for enterotype clustering.\n")

# ----------------------------- 4. Assign enterotypes ------------------------
pam_best <- pam(dist_mat, k = chosen_k)
enterotypes <- pam_best$clustering
sample_data(ps_filt)$Enterotype <- factor(enterotypes)

enterotype_list <- sample_data(ps_filt) %>%
  data.frame() %>%
  dplyr::select(SampleID, Enterotype, Group, Timepoint, SubjectID) %>%
  arrange(Enterotype, SampleID)
write.csv(enterotype_list, file.path(output_dir, "samples_per_enterotype.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

# ----------------------------- 5. Task 1: Group differences ----------------
cat("\n=== Task 1: Enterotype differences between groups ===\n")
cont_table <- table(sample_data(ps_filt)$Group, sample_data(ps_filt)$Enterotype)
cat("Contingency table:\n")
print(cont_table)

chisq_test <- chisq.test(cont_table)
cat("\nChi-square test:\n")
print(chisq_test)
write.csv(as.data.frame.matrix(cont_table), file.path(output_dir, "group_enterotype_contingency.csv"))

# Stacked bar plot
group_enterotype_prop <- prop.table(cont_table, margin = 1) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  setNames(c("Group", "Enterotype", "Proportion"))
p_bar <- ggplot(group_enterotype_prop, aes(x = Group, y = Proportion, fill = Enterotype)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack") +
  labs(title = "Enterotype distribution by group", y = "Proportion") +
  theme_minimal()
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "enterotype_by_group.pdf"), p_bar, width = 6, height = 4)
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "enterotype_by_group.png"), p_bar, width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 300)

# ----------------------------- 6. Task 2: Paired transitions ----------------
cat("\n=== Task 2: Enterotype changes between timepoints ===\n")

sample_data(ps_filt)$Timepoint <- as.character(sample_data(ps_filt)$Timepoint)

# Check pairing
pairing_check <- sample_data(ps_filt) %>%
  data.frame() %>%
  group_by(SubjectID) %>%
  summarise(n = n_distinct(Timepoint), times = paste(sort(unique(Timepoint)), collapse=","))
cat("Subjects with incomplete timepoints:\n")
print(pairing_check %>% filter(n != 2))

valid_subjects <- pairing_check %>% filter(n == 2) %>% pull(SubjectID)
ps_paired <- prune_samples(sample_data(ps_filt)$SubjectID %in% valid_subjects, ps_filt)
cat("Kept", nsamples(ps_paired), "samples from", length(valid_subjects), "subjects\n")

paired_df <- sample_data(ps_paired) %>%
  data.frame() %>%
  dplyr::select(SubjectID, Timepoint, Enterotype, Group, SampleID) %>%
  arrange(SubjectID, as.numeric(Timepoint))

paired_df$Timepoint_num <- as.numeric(paired_df$Timepoint)
time_order <- sort(unique(paired_df$Timepoint_num))  # e.g., -2, -1
time1_label <- paste0("Time ", time_order[1])
time2_label <- paste0("Time ", time_order[2])

wide_track <- paired_df %>%
  arrange(SubjectID, Timepoint_num) %>%
  group_by(SubjectID) %>%
  summarise(
    Group = first(Group),
    Enterotype_T1 = Enterotype[Timepoint_num == time_order[1]],
    Enterotype_T2 = Enterotype[Timepoint_num == time_order[2]],
    Sample_T1 = SampleID[Timepoint_num == time_order[1]],
    Sample_T2 = SampleID[Timepoint_num == time_order[2]]
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

cat("Subjects in paired analysis:", nrow(wide_track), "\n")
cat("Timepoint order: first =", time_order[1], ", second =", time_order[2], "\n")

# Transition matrix
transition <- table(wide_track$Enterotype_T1, wide_track$Enterotype_T2)
cat("\nTransition matrix (rows = first timepoint, cols = second):\n")
print(transition)

# Subjects that changed
changed <- wide_track[wide_track$Enterotype_T1 != wide_track$Enterotype_T2, ]
cat("Number of subjects that changed:", nrow(changed), 
    "(", round(100*nrow(changed)/nrow(wide_track),1), "%)\n")
write.csv(changed, file.path(output_dir, "samples_that_changed_enterotype.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

# Permutation test
observed_agreement <- sum(diag(transition)) / sum(transition)
n_perm <- 999
set.seed(123)
perm_agreement <- replicate(n_perm, {
  swapped <- wide_track
  for (i in 1:nrow(swapped)) {
    if (runif(1) > 0.5) {
      swapped[i, c("Enterotype_T1", "Enterotype_T2")] <- 
        swapped[i, c("Enterotype_T2", "Enterotype_T1")]
    }
  }
  tab <- table(swapped$Enterotype_T1, swapped$Enterotype_T2)
  sum(diag(tab)) / sum(tab)
})
p_value <- mean(perm_agreement <= observed_agreement)
cat("Permutation test p-value (change not random):", p_value, "\n")

# Prepare data for plots
trans_df <- as.data.frame(transition) %>%
  setNames(c("Enterotype_at_first", "Enterotype_at_second", "Count"))

# Heatmap (black text)
p_heatmap <- ggplot(trans_df, aes(x = Enterotype_at_first, y = Enterotype_at_second, fill = Count)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_text(aes(label = Count), color = "black", size = 5) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "steelblue") +
  labs(title = paste("Enterotype transitions:", time1_label, "→", time2_label),
       x = time1_label, y = time2_label) +
  theme_minimal()
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "enterotype_transitions_heatmap.pdf"), p_heatmap, width = 5, height = 4)

# Bar plot (grouped by first timepoint)
p_trans_bar <- ggplot(trans_df, aes(x = Enterotype_at_first, y = Count, fill = Enterotype_at_second)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  labs(title = paste("Transitions from", time1_label),
       x = paste("Enterotype at", time1_label),
       y = "Number of subjects",
       fill = paste("Enterotype at", time2_label)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  geom_text(aes(label = Count), position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), vjust = -0.5, size = 4)
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "enterotype_transitions_bars.pdf"), p_trans_bar, width = 6, height = 4)

# Sankey plot
sankey_df <- wide_track %>%
  count(Enterotype_T1, Enterotype_T2) %>%
  rename(Freq = n)
p_sankey <- ggplot(sankey_df,
                   aes(y = Freq, axis1 = Enterotype_T1, axis2 = Enterotype_T2)) +
  geom_alluvium(aes(fill = Enterotype_T1), width = 1/12) +
  geom_stratum(width = 1/12, fill = "grey85", color = "black") +
  geom_label(stat = "stratum", aes(label = after_stat(stratum))) +
  scale_x_discrete(limits = c(time1_label, time2_label), expand = c(0.05, 0.05)) +
  labs(title = "Enterotype transitions", y = "Number of subjects") +
  theme_minimal()
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "enterotype_transitions_sankey.pdf"), p_sankey, width = 7, height = 5)
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "enterotype_transitions_sankey.png"), p_sankey, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300)

# ----------------------------- 7. Save final phyloseq object ----------------
saveRDS(ps_filt, file.path(output_dir, "phyloseq_with_enterotypes.rds"))

# ----------------------------- 8. Session info -----------------------------
cat("\nAnalysis completed at:", date(), "\n")
#sessionInfo()
sink()
cat("\nAll results saved in folder:", output_dir, "\n")
```
